authoritative_sources.title: Businessperson's Guide to Federal Warranty Law
authoritative_sources.source_class: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FTC is the primary U.S. federal consumer-protection authority for warranty-law guidance. This manual explains Magnuson-Moss coverage, pre-sale availability, warranty dispute procedures, and where repair workmanship coverage enters the federal warranty framework.
used_for: pre-sale and customer-facing warranty record expectations
boundary between intake validation and final warranty adjudication

authoritative_sources.title: Warranties
authoritative_sources.source_class: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official FTC consumer guidance is authoritative for consumer-facing warranty terminology, including the difference between warranties and separately purchased service contracts or extended coverage.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: intake terminology normalization and consumer-rights framing

authoritative_sources.title: Using Credit Cards and Disputing Charges
authoritative_sources.source_class: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FTC consumer advice summarizes the Fair Credit Billing Act billing-error and dispute process, making it authoritative public guidance for payment mismatch, withheld-payment, and dispute-routing boundaries relevant to repair deposits and service fees.

authoritative_sources.title: Electronic and Appliance Repair Dealer FAQs
authoritative_sources.publisher: California Department of Consumer Affairs, Bureau of Household Goods and Services
authoritative_sources.source_class: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: California's repair-dealer regulator publishes official intake workflow guidance on written estimates, diagnosis fees, service-call charges, additional authorizations, service receipts or claim checks, and invoices. It is directly authoritative for a major U.S. repair-services jurisdiction and supplies concrete intake artifact expectations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: repair intake, estimate authorization, and claim-check handling

authoritative_sources.title: SB-244 Right to Repair Act
authoritative_sources.source_class: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official California statutory text is authoritative for parts and service-literature availability for electronic and appliance products, which affects whether an older device remains supportable for repair intake.

authoritative_sources.title: Recall Checklist
authoritative_sources.source_class: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CPSC is the primary U.S. federal regulator for consumer-product safety. Its recall checklist is authoritative public workflow guidance for when normal intake and customer-service handling must give way to recall and hazard escalation.

authoritative_sources.title: Recall Handbook
authoritative_sources.source_class: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The CPSC Recall Handbook is the official operating reference for recall communications, remedies, and corrective-action workflow, which constrains what customer-facing promises an intake specialist may or may not make in a safety case.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: customer-impact safety communication and escalation packaging

authoritative_sources.title: Guidelines for Media Sanitization (NIST SP 800-88r2)
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.source_class: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NIST is the authoritative U.S. standards body for information-security guidance. SP 800-88r2 is the recognized standard reference for data-bearing media handling and sanitization, which matters when phones, tablets, laptops, and smart devices enter repair workflows.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: data-bearing device intake and custody caveats
used_for: tenant-specific policy dependency for locked devices

authoritative_sources.title: Fighting Identity Theft with the Red Flags Rule: A How-To Guide for Business
authoritative_sources.source_class: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FTC business guidance is authoritative public fraud-control guidance for covered credit contexts and provides a practical public baseline for recognizing identity-mismatch and suspicious-account indicators that should route to fraud or loss-prevention owners.

workflow_stages.name: repair request intake and identity capture
workflow_stages.description: Capture customer, account, and product identifiers; verify claim-check posture; and establish whether the customer has authority to present the unit for repair.
source_classes: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance

workflow_stages.name: coverage, estimate, and authorization review
workflow_stages.description: Determine whether warranty, protection-plan, service-contract, diagnosis-fee, service-call-fee, and written-estimate prerequisites are satisfied before repair work proceeds.
source_classes: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references

workflow_stages.name: booking, payment, and queue-state validation
workflow_stages.description: Reconcile appointment, order, deposit, quoted-fee, and service-ticket records so the intake disposition uses an authoritative record-of-truth rather than an improvised customer-service answer.
source_classes: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: product-support and repairability screening
workflow_stages.description: Check model support, parts-availability expectations, in-scope repair category, and whether the product should be routed to a different service lane because supportability or service model differs.
source_classes: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references

workflow_stages.name: safety, recall, and data-handling stop review
workflow_stages.description: Screen for recalls, stop-use conditions, swelling batteries, electrical hazards, suspicious ownership mismatch, and data-bearing-device handling caveats before normal intake proceeds.
source_classes: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance

workflow_stages.name: acceptance, pend, rejection, or routing decision
workflow_stages.description: Issue the intake disposition and route to the correct adjacent specialist when pricing, refund, warranty, fraud, or safety authority determines the outcome.
source_classes: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance

artifacts: customer records, contact confirmations, and account notes
proof of purchase, invoice, warranty, protection-plan, and service-contract records
service tickets, appointment or booking records, and service-call dispatch notes
written estimates, diagnosis-fee disclosures, service-call-fee disclosures, and authorization records
service receipt or claim check, intake photos, accessory lists, and condition notes
payment deposits, credit-card dispute notes, and promotion or refund records
serial number, IMEI, model, SKU, and parts-support references
recall notices, stop-use flags, battery or electrical hazard indicators, and loss-prevention artifacts

allowed: Classify a repair request as in-scope, out-of-scope, blocked, or ready based on named intake prerequisites and authoritative records.
Validate minimum customer, product, estimate, booking, contract, and safety facts required before downstream diagnosis or repair begins.
Prepare evidence-backed intake disposition notes, prerequisite checklists, and routing packets without claiming downstream approval authority.
escalate: Pricing override, diagnosis-fee waiver, refund exception, promotion exception, or final financial outcome is required.
Warranty coverage, service-contract scope, out-of-support product handling, or policy interpretation changes the outcome and exceeds intake authority.
Recall, stop-use, swelling battery, electrical hazard, suspected stolen property, identity mismatch, or other fraud or safety factor determines the answer.
Public guidance and tenant policy conflict materially on estimate, payment, data-handling, or consumer-rights treatment.
refuse: Skip serial, identity, estimate-authorization, booking, or safety validation just to move work forward.
Approve downstream repair, refund, pricing, warranty, fraud, or safety outcomes instead of only validating intake conditions.
Treat missing or conflicting payment, ownership, or recall evidence as routine noise and continue ordinary intake.

domain_failure_modes: Accepting a device without serial, IMEI, or claim-check traceability and later being unable to reconcile ownership or custody.
Sending a unit to diagnosis or repair before written estimate or contract authorization is present.
Treating service contracts, extended coverage, and manufacturer warranties as interchangeable without checking the governing record.
Using the wrong record-of-truth when booking, service ticket, POS, and payment records conflict on quoted fees or service location.
Continuing ordinary intake on a recalled appliance or a swelling-battery device instead of escalating a customer-safety case.
Guessing at refund or diagnosis-fee exceptions when public guidance and tenant policy differ.
Absorbing suspected stolen-property or identity-mismatch cases as customer-service work instead of routing to fraud or loss-prevention.
Assuming a data-bearing device can be accepted, unlocked, or sanitized without tenant-specific policy and custody controls.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Refund or policy-exception request outside lane authority
Customer-impact issue with safety or fraud implications
Missing prerequisite or required intake data
Request routed to the wrong lane before execution starts
Attempt to bypass access, eligibility, or authorization checks

unresolved_ambiguities.ambiguity: Written-estimate, diagnosis-fee, cancellation, and implied-warranty rules vary by jurisdiction beyond the federal baseline.
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: May change financial or customer-rights handling for specific states or municipalities.
unresolved_ambiguities.required_follow_up: Retrieve tenant jurisdiction, posted policy, and local legal guidance before final fee or refund decisions.

unresolved_ambiguities.ambiguity: Data-bearing-device intake rules differ by tenant on passcode collection, activation-lock handling, and whether customer data must remain untouched.
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: May change whether a locked or uncleared device can be accepted for repair and who owns sanitization steps.
unresolved_ambiguities.required_follow_up: Retrieve tenant device-handling and privacy policy before accepting locked phones, tablets, laptops, or smart-home electronics.
